purpose: The complete worker agent progress reporting protocol — the canonical reference for how worker agents in a !p_track swarm report live state to the dashboard via per-task progress files at {tracker_root}/dashboards/{dashboardId}/progress/{task_id}.json. Defines the full progress JSON schema (status, started_at/completed_at, summary, stage, message, milestones[], deviations[], logs[], files_changed[], shared_context, sibling_reads, annotations, prompt_size, template_version, dashboard_id), the seven fixed lifecycle stages (reading_context → planning → implementing → testing → finalizing → completed/failed), the eight mandatory write points, hook-enforced quality rules (log count thresholds, vague message rejection, milestone requirements), atomic write semantics (full-file overwrite, sole-writer model, write-then-rename for shell), the partial-completion protocol (80%+ done = completed not failed), the optional PKI annotations subschema, and the sibling shared_context coordination fields. This is the worker's bible.
gotchas: {tracker_root} and {project_root} are DIFFERENT locations — code work happens in {project_root}, progress reporting goes to {tracker_root}. Confusing the two is the most common worker failure mode.
dashboard_id must be present in every progress write and must match the directory name — the server REJECTS writes where these don't match (dashboard binding enforcement).

Workers write the FULL file every time (overwrite, not append) — they are sole writers, no read-modify-write needed. Manual echo/cat shell writes are forbidden because they don't guarantee atomicity; use the Write tool or write-then-rename.

Hooks automatically validate every write: empty logs[], log messages <20 chars, vague placeholder messages ('Starting...', 'Done'), insufficient log count for stage (planning ≥2, implementing ≥3, testing ≥4, finalizing/completed ≥5), and consecutive duplicate messages will trigger warnings.
files_changed[] is REQUIRED from the implementing stage onward — must be updated incrementally as files change, not batched at the end. Paths are relative to {project_root}, not {tracker_root}.
On completion, files_changed must match the FILES CHANGED section of the return format — mismatches break the master's downstream prompt construction.
Partial completion with 80%+ done uses status 'completed' (not 'failed') with a clear blocker summary + MODERATE deviation. Reserving 'failed' for zero-useful-output cases is a deliberate convention.
Token estimate uses Math.ceil(totalChars / 3.5) — this 3.5 ratio (mixed content) must match agent/utils/token_estimate.js to be consistent.
annotations differ from files_changed: annotations are about files the worker READ deeply (PKI material), not files modified.
patterns: Full-file atomic overwrite via the Write tool (which uses tmp-rename internally) — the only correct way to update progress files.
Fixed seven-stage lifecycle progressed in order: reading_context → planning → implementing → testing → finalizing → completed/failed.
Eight mandatory write points: initial write before work, after reading upstream deps, on every stage transition, on every deviation, on every error, on every file change, on completion, on failure.
Append-only semantics for milestones[], deviations[], logs[], files_changed[] (no removal once added).
Annotations sub-schema (gotchas/patterns/conventions per file path) intentionally mirrors PKI per-file annotation structure for clean merging.
Log levels follow strict color mapping: info=purple, warn=lime/yellow, error=red, deviation=yellow.
conventions: Log entry shape: { at, level, msg }; milestone shape: { at, msg }; deviation shape: { at, severity, description }.
files_changed entry: { path, action: 'created'|'modified'|'deleted' } with paths relative to {project_root}.
template_version tracks the dispatch prompt template (e.g., 'p_track_v2') for master-side calibration.
summary field must be specific and quantified ('Created auth middleware with rate limiting — 3 endpoints'), never vague ('Done').
